benchmark_name: Can LLMs Inhibit Optimization in Supply-Chain Decision-Making?
answer_correctness_justification: Ordering decisions evaluated against deterministic game mechanics. Fully verifiable.
construct_validity_justification: Beer Game simulation directly measures cognitive biases in supply chain decision-making. Strong alignment with Zhu & Heydari research.
code_quality_justification: Full simulation environment with proper game mechanics. Well-organized and documented.
sample_size_justification: 20-50 rounds per simulation generating >20 decision points.
metric_design_justification: Multiple distinct metrics: total cost, bullwhip effect, order variance, per-echelon breakdown. Multi-dimensional.
problem_statement_justification: Compelling gap analysis: existing benchmarks don't test cognitive biases in sequential multi-agent decisions.
technical_documentation_justification: Comprehensive documentation including game mechanics, parameters, demand patterns, academic references.
capability_targeting_justification: Targets inhibitory control in sequential optimization under delayed feedback. Natural experimental controls.
item_diversity_justification: Structured sub-conditions: 4 echelons × demand patterns × time horizons. Systematic variation.
